message:
feat: am runner + MCP base-path alias + conformance parity

- Add scripts/am dev runner + README quick start

- Accept /api <-> /mcp HTTP base-path aliases + tests

- Conformance parity fixes (empty queries, identity resource, renew clamp)

- Regenerate python_reference fixtures

 {"id":"bd-179","title":"MCP Agent Mail Rust: 100% feature parity + conformance + benchmarks","description":"Goal: complete the Rust port of legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code with 100% behavior/feature coverage, proven by fixture-based conformance tests + benchmarks (similar standard as rich_rust/beads_rust).

Non-negotiables:
- Prefer local crates over upstream ecosystem where applicable:
  - MCP: /dp/fastmcp_rust
  - SQLite: /dp/sqlmodel_rust
  - IO/concurrency: /dp/asupersync (avoid tokio unless unavoidable)
  - Console UI: /dp/frankentui
  - Beads: /dp/beads_rust
  - Agent detection: /dp/coding_agent_session_search
- No destructive commands or file deletions without explicit permission.

Acceptance criteria:
-  passes

+{"id":"br-10wc","title":"[epic] AgentMailTUI: full frankentui-native interactive operations console","description":"## Background\nPrevious work delivered a rich startup banner and inline HUD in the HTTP server. The next step is a true standalone TUI experience (showcase quality) that makes mcp_agent_mail not found in ~/mcp_agent_mail feel like a first-class operations cockpit, not just decorated logs.\n\n## Vision\nBuild AgentMailTUI as a real multi-pane app inspired by  showcase patterns: smooth navigation, dense but legible telemetry, live event flow, searchable history, and direct operational actions.\n\n## Product Goals\n- Make mcp_agent_mail not found in ~/mcp_agent_mail the easiest and most compelling way to run, observe, and operate MCP Agent Mail.\n- Consolidate message activity, tool calls, reservations, contact status, and system health in one interactive surface.\n- Support both MCP and API transport modes with seamless operator ergonomics.\n- Default to polished, information-rich UX while preserving power-user controls and performance.\n\n## Non-Goals\n- Do not remove existing server/web functionality.\n- Do not ship novelty visuals that hurt clarity, accessibility, or throughput.\n- Do not regress testability or deterministic output in CI/PTY tests.\n\n## Architecture Direction\n- Reuse and extend existing frankentui integration rather than inventing a parallel UI stack.\n- Introduce a typed event stream model feeding dashboard/search/inspect panes.\n- Use bounded buffers, incremental query paths, and virtualized rendering for sustained responsiveness.\n\n## Required Quality Bar\n- Comprehensive unit tests + PTY/E2E scenarios with detailed structured logging artifacts.\n- Clear dependency layering across beads so parallel implementation is safe.\n- Documentation/runbooks sufficient for new contributors to ship changes without tribal context.\n\n## Deliverable\nA complete, staged implementation plan (children of this epic) covering architecture, UX, data flow, connector modes (MCP/API), performance, testing, rollout, and docs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:12:19.615580143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:12:19.615580143Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-1bm","title":"HTTP Server Parity: Legacy Python Exact Match","description":"## Objective
Port the **HTTP server stack** to Rust with exact legacy Python behavior: auth, rate limiting, streamable HTTP transport, mail UI endpoints, logging, and background workers.

## Scope
- Middleware: bearer auth, JWT + RBAC, rate limiting.
- Streamable HTTP MCP transport (stateless ASGI adapter behavior, header normalization, base path mounting).
- Mail UI routes and static assets.
- Request logging + OTEL no‑op parity.
- Background workers (ack TTL, file reservation cleanup, tool metrics, retention/quota).
- Health + well‑known endpoints.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests per subsystem (JWT, rate limit, streamable HTTP, mail UI, logging, workers).
- E2E HTTP suite (br-2ei.9.6) covering auth, CORS, streamable, and UI endpoints.

## Logging/Artifacts
- HTTP transcripts + diffs under `tests/artifacts/http/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:32.057076856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646906905Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646884493Z","close_reason":"All HTTP server parity child beads complete (auth/JWT/RBAC, rate limit, peer addr/localhost bypass, streamable HTTP, /mail SSR UI, CORS, logging+OTEL no-op, workers, health/well-known)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:48:14.330942304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-36w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:23.374551664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1","title":"JWT Auth Parity (HS256 + JWKS + role claim)","description":"Objective:
Implement JWT authentication exactly as in legacy Python (mcp_agent_mail/http.py). This includes HS256 HMAC and JWKS verification paths, audience/issuer validation, role extraction, and error semantics. Must be 1:1 on outputs and failure modes.

Legacy Reference Behavior (must match):
- If JWT enabled and Authorization missing or not Bearer: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Decode token using:
  - If JWKS URL set: fetch JWKS, select key by header.kid, verify
  - Else if secret set: verify with HMAC secret
  - Algorithms from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default HS256)
- Validate audience/issuer if configured; otherwise ignore
- On any failure: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Extract roles from claim HTTP_JWT_ROLE_CLAIM (default "role")
  - String -> singleton set
  - List/tuple -> set of strings
  - Else -> empty
- If roles empty, use HTTP_RBAC_DEFAULT_ROLE
- Store claims for rate limit identity (sub) and any downstream uses

Implementation Considerations:
- JWKS fetch must be async-safe via asupersync HTTP client (no tokio)
- Cache JWKS by URL with TTL to avoid per-request latency
- Must not change external response schema or status codes
- Log auth failures at debug/trace (no extra HTTP output)

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
Unit tests:
- Missing Authorization header -> 401
- Non-Bearer Authorization -> 401
- HS256 valid/invalid signatures
- JWKS valid/invalid (kid missing, bad signature)
- Audience/issuer mismatch -> 401
- Role claim variants (string, list, missing)
Integration tests:
- Start server with HTTP_JWT_* env vars; make requests with valid/invalid JWT
- Verify RBAC path uses roles correctly
E2E:
- Covered by `br-1bm.1.5` (uses shared harness `br-2ei.9.1`).

Notes:
- Localhost bypass logic is owned by `br-1bm.3` + `br-1bm.8`; JWT must still match legacy in the non-bypass path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JWT behavior matches legacy for all vectors extracted in `br-1bm.1.1`.
- Role extraction matches Python behavior across all supported claim shapes.
- `br-1bm.1.5` unit+integration+E2E suite passes and produces high-signal artifacts on failure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperBarn","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:46.671284098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957613837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957588740Z","close_reason":"All subtasks br-1bm.1.1-1.5 complete; JWT unit+integration+E2E coverage passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1.1","title":"JWT Parity: extract legacy behavior + test vectors","description":"Purpose:
Extract exact legacy JWT behavior and define concrete test vectors so Rust matches Python 1:1.

Legacy Implementation Notes (from http.py):
- Header parse: token.split(".",1)[0]; base64url decode with padding; JSON parse. Any error -> None.
- If jwks_url set:
  - httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5) GET jwks_url -> .json()
  - JsonWebKey.import_key_set(jwks)
  - kid = header.get("kid"); key = find_by_kid(kid) if present else key_set.keys[0]
- Else if secret set:
  - JsonWebKey.import_key(secret, {"kty":"oct"})
- If key None -> Unauthorized.
- jwt.decode(token, key) using algs from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default ["HS256"]). Any error -> Unauthorized.
- If audience configured: claims.validate_aud(audience)
- If issuer configured: if str(claims.get("iss") or "") != issuer -> Unauthorized (no fallback)
- claims.validate() (handles exp/nbf/iat if present)
- Returns dict(claims) on success.

Concrete Test Vectors (must implement):
1) Missing Authorization header -> 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
2) Authorization not Bearer -> 401
3) Header parse fail (malformed base64 / non-JSON header) -> 401
4) JWKS path:
   - jwks_url set, kid present, matching key -> success
   - jwks_url set, kid present, no matching key -> 401
   - jwks_url set, kid missing -> uses first key in key_set
   - jwks fetch fails / invalid JSON -> 401
5) Secret path:
   - jwt_secret set, HS256 valid -> success
   - jwt_secret set, signature invalid -> 401
6) Algorithms:
   - alg not in HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS -> 401
7) Audience:
   - jwt_audience set, token aud matches -> success
   - jwt_audience set, token aud mismatch -> 401
8) Issuer:
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss matches exactly -> success
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss missing or different -> 401
9) Claims validate:
   - exp in past -> 401
   - nbf in future -> 401
   - exp/nbf absent -> ok
10) Role claim:
   - role claim string -> roles={string}
   - role claim list -> roles={strings}
   - role claim missing/invalid -> roles empty -> default role applied downstream

Deliverable:
- A markdown or JSON matrix of inputs -> expected HTTP status/body and roles extracted.
- Must be explicit enough that Rust tests can be derived directly.

Dependencies: none (spec extraction only).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:09.570324403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060998320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060980056Z","close_reason":"Extracted legacy JWT behavior + explicit test vectors in bead description","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-36w","title":"Config parity: .env loading + env var coverage","description":"## Objective
Match legacy configuration behavior, including `.env` loading and full env var coverage.

## Scope
- `.env` loading behavior (python‑decouple parity):
  - if .env missing → empty repository fallback; env vars only.
  - canonical env var set only if missing when alias present (LLM bridge).
- Ensure all env vars from spec are supported:
  - WORKTREES_ENABLED + GIT_IDENTITY_ENABLED gate
  - ALLOW_ABSOLUTE_ATTACHMENT_PATHS
  - TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED, LOG_RICH_ENABLED
  - NOTIFICATIONS_* defaults
  - HTTP_* (CORS/JWT/RBAC/rate limit)
  - LLM_* defaults
- CSV parsing for list settings (roles, tools, clusters).
- Match default values exactly.

## Tests
- Unit tests for .env presence/absence handling.
- Table‑driven env parsing tests for bools/ints/CSV lists.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store parsed config snapshots under `tests/artifacts/config/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Rust config matches legacy defaults and parsing semantics.
2. `.env` loading behavior matches python‑decouple (graceful missing file).
3. All env variables from spec are supported and tested.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:09.094825172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832107234Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832088128Z","close_reason":"Implemented .env loading + env var coverage + tests; checks blocked by sqlmodel-sqlite duplicate defs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-36w","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:12.895192349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3gs","title":"Share: sign manifest + encrypt bundle + deterministic ZIP","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 13–15: Ed25519 signing, age encryption, and deterministic ZIP packaging.

## Scope
- **sign_manifest**: sign manifest.json with Ed25519 seed; optionally output public key.
- **encrypt_bundle**: age encryption when recipients provided; output `.age` file; retain clear bundle if required by legacy.
- **package_directory_as_zip**: deterministic ZIP (stable ordering, timestamps, compression settings).

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and age encrypt/decrypt.
- Integration tests for deterministic ZIP hash.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store signatures, public keys, and zip hashes under `tests/artifacts/share/crypto/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Manifest signatures validate correctly with provided public key.
2. Encryption outputs readable bundles via `share decrypt`.
3. ZIP packaging is deterministic (stable hash across runs).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:36.383969609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.811535263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.811510687Z","close_reason":"Verified sign/encrypt/zip implementation via mcp-agent-mail-share tests (age roundtrip + deterministic zip)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3gs","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:41.980704900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"br-3gs","author":"CopperOwl","text":"Claimed br-3gs.

Plan:
- Audit existing share crypto + deterministic ZIP implementation in crates/mcp-agent-mail-share for legacy parity gaps.
- Add/adjust tests (signature verify, age encrypt/decrypt, deterministic ZIP hash) and required tests/artifacts/share/crypto/<timestamp>/ artifacts.
- Run gates (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-share) and close br-3gs.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:38:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3h94","title":"Error type mapping parity (CAPABILITY_DENIED, NOT_FOUND, etc.)","description":"## Objective
Ensure error type mapping and JSON error shapes match legacy across tools/resources.

## Scope
- Error codes list: `CAPABILITY_DENIED`, `NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, `TYPE_ERROR`, `MISSING_FIELD`, `DATABASE_POOL_EXHAUSTED`, `TIMEOUT`, `GIT_INDEX_LOCK`, `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED`, `CONTACT_REQUIRED`, `CONTACT_BLOCKED`, `OS_ERROR`, `DATABASE_ERROR`, `RESOURCE_BUSY`, `PERMISSION_ERROR`, `CONNECTION_ERROR`, `UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION`.
- Map internal errors to these codes consistently.
- Ensure tool/resource responses include legacy error fields and HTTP error status where applicable.

## Tests
- Unit tests for error mapping table.
- Conformance tests for representative error cases (missing fields, invalid args, capability denied, not found).

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store error responses under `tests/artifacts/errors/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All error codes and shapes match legacy.
2. Conformance tests include error cases for each category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:30.739623609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:17:59.988606898Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:17:59.988584837Z","close_reason":"completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h94","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:35.625592551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-3np","title":"CLI: share export/update parity (interactive + defaults)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share export` and `share update` commands with legacy behavior (including `--interactive`).

## Scope
- Flags: `--output/-o`, `--interactive/-i`, `--project/-p` (repeat), thresholds, scrub preset, chunk settings, `--dry-run`, `--zip`, `--signing-key`, `--signing-public-out`, `--age-recipient` (repeat).
- Defaults: inline=64KiB, detach=25MiB, chunk threshold=20MiB, chunk size=4MiB; scrub preset `standard`.
- Validation: invalid preset exits 1; enforce chunk size >=1024; auto‑adjust detach threshold if <= inline.
- `share export`:
  - `--dry-run` creates temp output + prints summary only.
  - `--interactive` wizard prompts and overrides flags.
- `share update`:
  - loads config from existing `manifest.json` and stored export config.
  - default `--zip` is **false** (contrast export default true).

## Tests
- Unit tests for flag parsing + defaults.
- Integration tests with temp bundle export/update; verify manifest config updates.
- Golden output for `--dry-run` summary.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store CLI outputs under `tests/artifacts/cli/share/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share export/update match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. interactive mode overrides flags and produces identical outputs to legacy.
3. dry-run produces no bundle artifacts but prints expected summary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:51.526681473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:02:13.427048743Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:02:13.427025710Z","close_reason":"Implemented share export/update parity (interactive wizard + defaults + dry-run summary + update sync + signing/zip/age warnings) and verified via cargo test gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3np","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:33.896970708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3np","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:56.127446696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"br-3np","author":"PearlOwl","text":"Progress: implemented  wizard + real  summary; ensured export pipeline copies viewer assets; fixed export snapshot finalization to rebuild  when schema mismatched and to drop legacy FTS triggers so message updates don’t fail. Manual dry-run against repo  now succeeds.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:52:17Z"},{"id":6,"issue_id":"br-3np","author":"PearlOwl","text":"Progress details (no backticks): share export interactive wizard implemented; real dry-run prints summary without writing to output; export now copies viewer assets; share snapshot finalization rebuilds fts_messages when schema mismatched and drops legacy FTS triggers so later UPDATE messages operations do not fail. Manual dry-run against ./storage.sqlite3 succeeds.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:52:34Z"}]}

2. Manifests include correct hashes and metadata.
3.  {"id":"br-9jl","title":"CLI: share verify/decrypt parity (signature + age)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share verify` and `share decrypt` with legacy semantics.

## Scope
- `share verify`:
  - Validate SRI hashes and optional Ed25519 signature.
  - `--public-key` uses provided base64 key; else use manifest signature.
- `share decrypt`:
  - `--output` defaults to filename sans `.age` (or `_decrypted`).
  - `--identity` (age key file) is mutually exclusive with `--passphrase`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and decrypt flag validation.
- Integration tests decrypting a bundle produced by share export.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store verification results under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_verify/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share verify/decrypt match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. Signature validation and age decrypt succeed on valid inputs and fail loudly on invalid.
3. Mutual‑exclusion flag handling matches legacy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDune","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:17.420980860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917640762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917621787Z","close_reason":"Added CLI integration tests for share verify/decrypt parity; validated exit codes + defaults","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:44.284948962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:23.973796081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-ave","title":"Share: sqlite snapshot + project scope","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 1–2: `create_sqlite_snapshot` and `apply_project_scope` with exact legacy semantics.

## Scope
- **create_sqlite_snapshot**:
  - Resolve destination path; create parent dirs.
  - Error if destination exists (never overwrite).
  - Open source DB; run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)` when enabled.
  - Use sqlite backup API to copy source → destination.
- **apply_project_scope**:
  - Load projects (id, slug, human_key); error if none.
  - If identifiers empty → no deletions.
  - Match identifiers case‑insensitive by slug or human_key; error on unknown.
  - Delete order per spec (agent_links, sibling_suggestions, messages + recipients, file_reservations, agents, projects).

## Tests
- Unit tests for identifier matching + error cases.
- Integration tests with temp DB and multiple projects; verify deletions and counts.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Save before/after row counts under `tests/artifacts/share/snapshot/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Snapshot creation refuses overwrite and produces byte‑identical DB backup.
2. Project scoping deletes only disallowed project data in the specified order.
3. Tests are deterministic and isolated to temp DB paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:05.665286231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363640110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363620834Z","close_reason":"Implemented backup snapshot + scope parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ave","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:11.619634150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"br-hk3v","title":"Dev UX: am runner script + /api<->/mcp HTTP path alias","description":"## Problem
Running the local HTTP server currently requires multiple manual exports (bearer token from a separate envfile, HTTP_PATH override, LOG_RICH_ENABLED) and is error-prone. In this environment some clients expect /mcp/ while the server defaults to /api/.

## Goals
- One easy entrypoint: am serve starts the server with sane defaults and rich console enabled by default.
- Auto-load HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from a dev envfile (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env) if not already set.
- Support BOTH /api/ and /mcp/ for the MCP HTTP endpoint (alias each other so either client works).
- Keep backward compatibility for existing E2E scripts that hardcode /api/.

## Implementation
1) Add scripts/am shell wrapper:
- am serve (default) runs: cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
- Defaults: HTTP_PATH=/mcp/ and LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
- Options: --path mcp|api, --env-file <path>, --host, --port, --no-auth
- If HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN is unset, attempt to read it from env file (strip quotes).

2) Server: accept /api and /mcp as synonyms for the MCP base path:
- If configured base is /api, also accept requests under /mcp by rewriting the request path to the configured base before passing to HttpRequestHandler (configured with base_path=config.http_path).
- If configured base is /mcp, also accept /api similarly.
- Do not change behavior for arbitrary nested bases (for example /api/mcp).

3) Docs: update README.md with scripts/am usage and the /api + /mcp alias note.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the alias path logic (path_allowed + canonicalization).

## Acceptance Criteria
- scripts/am serve works without manual exports in a typical dev setup (token file present).
- With default config (HTTP_PATH unset), both POST /api/ and POST /mcp/ reach the MCP handler.
- Full quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.

Running the local HTTP server currently requires multiple manual exports (bearer token from a separate envfile, HTTP_PATH override, LOG_RICH_ENABLED) and is error-prone. In this environment some clients expect /mcp/ while the server defaults to /api/.

## Goals
- One easy entrypoint: am serve starts the server with sane defaults and rich console enabled by default.
- Auto-load HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from a dev envfile (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env) if not already set.
- Support BOTH /api/ and /mcp/ for the MCP HTTP endpoint (alias each other so either client works).
- Keep backward compatibility for existing E2E scripts that hardcode /api/.

## Implementation
1) Add scripts/am shell wrapper:
- am serve (default) runs: cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
- Defaults: HTTP_PATH=/mcp/ and LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
- Options: --path mcp|api, --env-file <path>, --host, --port, --no-auth
- If HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN is unset, attempt to read it from env file (strip quotes).

2) Server: accept /api and /mcp as synonyms for the MCP base path:
- If configured base is /api, also accept requests under /mcp by rewriting the request path to the configured base before passing to HttpRequestHandler (configured with base_path=config.http_path).
- If configured base is /mcp, also accept /api similarly.
- Do not change behavior for arbitrary nested bases (for example /api/mcp).

3) Docs: update README.md with scripts/am usage and the /api + /mcp alias note.

## Tests

 ## Quick Start
 
+### Easiest Local Run (`am`)
+```bash
+cd /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust
+alias am='/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/am'
+am serve
+```
+
+What `am serve` does by default:
+- sets `HTTP_PATH=/mcp/`
+- sets `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true`
+- auto-loads `HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN` from `~/mcp_agent_mail/.env` if not already set
+
 ### Run MCP Server (stdio)
 ```bash
 cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail
@@ -16,6 +28,10 @@ cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail
 cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
 ```
 
+Notes:
+- The server now accepts both `/mcp/` and `/api/` as MCP HTTP base-path aliases for local/dev compatibility.
+- `scripts/am` options: `--path mcp|api`, `--host`, `--port`, `--env-file`, `--no-auth`.
+
 ### Run CLI
 ```bash
 cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail-cli -- --help

@@ -0,0 +1,135 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+repo_root="$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)"
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'EOF'
+Usage: am [serve] [options] [-- <extra args for `mcp-agent-mail serve`>]
+
+Convenience runner for local MCP Agent Mail development.
+Defaults:
+- HTTP_PATH=/mcp/
+- LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
+- host=127.0.0.1, port=8765
+- loads HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env if not set
+
+Options:
+  --host <host>          Server host (default: 127.0.0.1)
+  --port <port>          Server port (default: 8765)
+  --path <mcp|api|/x/>   Base path (default: mcp)
+  --env-file <path>      Env file for HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env)
+  --no-auth              Unset HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN for this run
+  -h, --help             Show help
+
+Examples:
+  scripts/am serve
+  scripts/am --path api
+  scripts/am --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9000
+EOF
+}
+
+normalize_http_path() {
+  local value="$1"
+  case "$value" in
+    mcp|/mcp|/mcp/) echo "/mcp/" ;;
+    api|/api|/api/) echo "/api/" ;;
+    *)
+      if [[ "$value" != /* ]]; then
+        value="/$value"
+      fi
+      if [[ "$value" != */ ]]; then
+        value="$value/"
+      fi
+      echo "$value"
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+read_env_value() {
+  local file="$1"
+  local key="$2"
+  if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
+    return 1
+  fi
+  command grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${key}=" "$file" | tail -n1 | cut -d= -f2-
+}
+
+if [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; then
+  case "$1" in
+    serve) shift ;;
+    help|-h|--help)
+      usage
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+  esac
+fi
+
+host="127.0.0.1"
+port="8765"
+path_mode="mcp"
+env_file="${HOME}/mcp_agent_mail/.env"
+no_auth=0
+extra_args=()
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --host)
+      host="${2:-}"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --port)
+      port="${2:-}"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --path)
+      path_mode="${2:-}"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --env-file)
+      env_file="${2:-}"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --no-auth)
+      no_auth=1
+      shift
+      ;;
+    -h|--help)
+      usage
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    --)
+      shift
+      extra_args=("$@")
+      break
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
+      usage >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+export HTTP_PATH="$(normalize_http_path "$path_mode")"
+export LOG_RICH_ENABLED="${LOG_RICH_ENABLED:-true}"
+
+if [[ "$no_auth" -eq 1 ]]; then
+  unset HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN
+else
+  if [[ -z "${HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+    if token="$(read_env_value "$env_file" "HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN" 2>/dev/null)"; then
+      token="$(printf '%s' "$token" | sed -e 's/^[[:space:]]*//' -e 's/[[:space:]]*$//')"
+      token="$(printf '%s' "$token" | sed -e 's/^"//' -e 's/"$//' -e "s/^'//" -e "s/'$//")"
+      if [[ -n "$token" ]]; then
+        export HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN="$token"
+      fi
+    fi
+  fi
+fi
+
+cd "$repo_root"
+echo "am: host=${host} port=${port} path=${HTTP_PATH} rich=${LOG_RICH_ENABLED}" >&2
+
+exec cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host "$host" --port "$port" "${extra_args[@]}"
